content_gaps: Most pages focus on one or two cultures (e.g., Tibetan Buddhism or Native American tribes) but lack a comprehensive global overview. A page that synthesizes meanings from Paleolithic times, Indigenous American, Maori, Tibetan, and ancient Egyptian cultures would be more thorough.

There is very little information on the specific meanings of different types of animal bones (e.g., yak vs. buffalo vs. whale). Most content speaks generally about 'bone'. Detailing the unique symbolism of bones from different animals (strength of the yak, prosperity from the buffalo, guidance from the whale) would add significant value.

The ethical sourcing of bone for jewelry is a significant concern for modern buyers but is only briefly touched upon by a few sites. A detailed section on how to identify ethically sourced bone (e.g., from animals that died naturally, byproducts of the food industry) versus unethically sourced materials would address a key buyer question.

Many articles discuss historical and cultural significance but fail to connect these meanings to contemporary jewelry design and modern fashion. An analysis of how modern artists and designers are reinterpreting traditional bone jewelry would provide a fresh and relevant angle.

The concept of 'mana' or the spirit of the wearer being absorbed by the bone is a powerful and recurring theme, particularly in Maori and Hawaiian cultures, but it is not fully explained. A deeper dive into this animist belief—how the bone changes color and becomes a spiritual heirloom—would be a compelling and unique content angle.
